sections.description: Define and protect network boundaries for systems that handle or route payment activity.

questions.question: Are network security controls (such as firewalls or routers) documented and in place between your payment environment and the internet?
questions.help_text: Applies to IP-connected terminals; dial-only setups may differ—answer for what you actually use.

questions.question: Are rules for allowed traffic reviewed at least when the network changes or at least annually?
questions.help_text: Keeps only necessary connections active.

questions.question: Are administrative interfaces for routers, firewalls, or terminals protected from open internet access (VPN, jump host, or local only)?
questions.help_text: Reduces remote takeover risk.

questions.question: Have you defined which networks and devices are in scope for PCI versus general business use?
questions.help_text: Helps auditors understand your card data paths.

questions.question: Are inbound and outbound connections restricted to what your payment application needs?
questions.help_text: Default deny is the goal.

questions.question: If wireless is used in or near the payment path, is it secured and separated appropriately?
questions.help_text: Not Applicable if no wireless touches payment traffic.

sections.description: Harden systems and software against unsafe defaults and misconfiguration.

questions.question: Do you follow written configuration standards for terminals and any in-scope network devices?
questions.help_text: Include removing defaults and unnecessary services.

questions.question: Do you keep an up-to-date list of payment terminals and which sites or registers they belong to?
questions.help_text: Helps with replacements and audits.

questions.question: Are backups of settlement or terminal configuration data encrypted or otherwise protected from unauthorized access?
questions.help_text: Applies if you back up payment-related systems.

sections.description: Collect, store, and dispose of card data only as your business truly needs.

questions.question: Do you avoid storing full card numbers, magnetic stripe, or CVV on your systems after authorization?
questions.help_text: SAQ B assumes you do not store cardholder data electronically.

questions.question: If paper receipts exist, are they handled and destroyed in a way that limits exposure of full card numbers?
questions.help_text: Truncation or secure disposal is typical.

sections.description: Protect card data in motion across networks and manage keys responsibly.

questions.question: Is cardholder data encrypted with strong cryptography when sent over open or public networks?
questions.help_text: Often validated through your processor’s terminal program.

questions.question: If you manage encryption keys for payment traffic, are they stored and rotated using approved practices?
questions.help_text: Often handled by your processor—Not Applicable if you never touch keys.

sections.description: Prevent, detect, and respond to malicious software on in-scope systems.

questions.question: Are PCs or servers that manage terminals or settlement protected against malware where applicable?
questions.help_text: Choose Not Applicable if no such systems exist.

questions.question: Are APIs or file transfers to your processor authenticated and encrypted end-to-end?
questions.help_text: No clear-text credentials in batch uploads.

sections.description: Control code and configuration changes and keep components patched.

questions.question: Are changes to payment terminals or network gear approved and tracked?
questions.help_text: Even a simple ticket or sign-off process counts.

sections.description: Grant access to systems and data on a need-to-know basis.

questions.question: Is access to terminal settings and back-office tools limited to people who need it?
questions.help_text: Includes vendor remote access if used.

questions.question: Is remote access for vendors or admins into payment systems authenticated strongly and time-limited?
questions.help_text: Unique credentials per vendor rep; disable when not needed.

sections.description: Verify users and administrators before granting access.

questions.question: Does each person use their own login for systems that can change payment settings?
questions.help_text: No shared passwords for admin tasks.

sections.description: Protect devices and facilities that can reach cardholder environments.

questions.question: Are terminals and any in-scope devices protected from tampering or theft (for example, secured counters or cables)?
questions.help_text: Basic physical care for payment devices.

sections.description: Keep useful audit trails and review them for suspicious activity.

questions.question: Do you capture audit logs for access to payment systems where logging is available, and protect logs from tampering?
questions.help_text: May be limited on some terminals—answer for systems you control.

questions.question: Do you run external vulnerability scans or penetration tests for internet-facing payment systems as required?
questions.help_text: Coordinate with your acquirer or ASV program.

sections.description: Find and fix weaknesses in systems and applications in scope.

questions.question: Do you patch or replace software on systems you manage that could affect payment security?
questions.help_text: Includes back-office machines touching settlement.

questions.question: Do you monitor or detect suspicious network activity where feasible (IDS/IPS or equivalent)?
questions.help_text: Scale to your size; document what you use.

questions.question: Do you have a simple incident response plan if terminals or networks are suspected compromised?
questions.help_text: Who to call and what to preserve.

sections.description: Document expectations, vendors, incidents, and ongoing risk awareness.

questions.question: Do you maintain a security policy covering payment devices and related responsibilities?
questions.help_text: Short and current is fine.

questions.question: Do you track processors and vendors that support your payment terminals and review their PCI status as needed?
questions.help_text: Keep agreements or attestations on file.

questions.question: Do employees who handle terminals or payment issues get basic security awareness training yearly?
questions.help_text: Phishing and safe handling of cardholder information.

questions.question: Are DNS and email for in-scope staff protected against phishing and malware delivery where feasible?
questions.help_text: DMARC/SPF and safe DNS resolvers reduce compromise risk.
